title: Isolation of genomic DNA from Aiptasia polyps
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7flhs","text":"This protocol is for isolation of genomic DNA from Aiptasia (Exaiptasia diaphana) polyps. It works with both symbiotic and aposymbiotic polyps of medium size and produces DNA suitable for PCRs. The final yield depends on the size and number of anemones used but should generally lie around 50-100 ng/µL in 50 µL volume.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dpqek","text":"Use general good practices for molecular biology lab work to keep the working area clean and the working reagents and samples free of possible contaminants. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3vr5k","text":"If you are unfamiliar with the protocol (and especially the phenol-chloroform extraction) test it on a range of sizes of dispensable anemones first.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"q19c","text":"Pre-heat a water bath to 65°C and thaw the 2×CTAB buffer in it until all CTAB precipitate has dissolved.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ijc4","text":"Set a water bath or heat block to 37°C. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"dqaog","text":"Safety Remarks:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9osgp","text":"Be sure to follow chemical safety procedures. β-mercaptoethanol and phenol are very toxic if inhaled, ingested or by skin contact. CTAB is irritant, toxic on ingestion and harmful to aquatic life.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a1jeo","text":"So, read the safety data sheets, work under a hood for all steps involving open tubes with CTAB, phenol and chloroform, wear nitrile gloves (don’t re-use them), safety glasses and a lab coat. If you never worked with phenol:chloroform before, ask someone who did about handling of it. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":141,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jtsu","text":"Discard bottom organic layers into a phenol-chloroform liquid waste container, the tubes and glass beads into a solid phenol-chloroform waste container and dispose of according to your institutions guidelines.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"di5tv","text":"Discard CTAB and ethanol waste according to your institutions guidelines.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

(1992) Marine Biology 114: 317-325):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14},{"style":"bold","offset":66,"length":1},{"style":"italic","offset":14,"length":53}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d7v4e","text":"End concentrations:                                                          For 100 ml buffer:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19},{"style":"bold","offset":77,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b55em","text":"1.4 M               NaCl                                                             28 mL of a 5 M stock solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ekkjo","text":"20 mM            EDTA (pH 8)                                                 4 mL of a 0.5 M stock solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8s261","text":"100 mM          Tris-Cl (pH 8)                                               10 mL of a 1 M stock solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f5pi3","text":"2% (w/v)        CTAB powder                                               2 g","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"146fr","text":"                        ddH2O                                                          Top off to 95 mL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":27,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ect2","text":"Warm to 65°C under stirring to bring the CTAB into solution. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d5h7m","text":"Add water to bring final volume to 100 mL.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"evpoq","text":"Pass through a 0.22-µm sterile filter with a PVDF or PES membrane.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"frgta","text":"Make two 50-mL aliquots and store at −20°C.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ltmr","text":"Heat to 65°C before usage since freezing leads precipitation of the CTAB.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8vjed","text":"In a chemical hood:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"asglf","text":"Before first usage, add 100 µL β-mercaptoethanol (TOXIC!!!) to the 50-mL aliquot [0.2% (v/v) end concentration].","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":50,"length":8},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":50,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aj92h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"304F32044A4911F094480A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.sai-infusion.com/products/blunt-needles?variant=333055841"}}}}
steps: In a chemical hood: Homogenize one anemone (oral disc diameter 2-5 mm) with a disposable plastic pestle in 600 µL 2× CTAB buffer individually in 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tubes. Check tight fit of the pestle into tube before starting.

In a chemical hood: Transfer homogenate into a new screw-cap or safe-lock 1.5 or 2-mL microcentrifuge tube containing ~200 µL of glass beads (0.5 mm). Tight closing of the tube is important for the phenol extraction; simple tubes might leak! Make sure your tubes tolerate the chemicals (phenol, chloroform, ethanol) and centrifugation forces (≥16,000 ×g), especially if using a non-cooled centrifuge in which the tubes can get really warm (it's best to use a refrigerated centrifuge set to 25°C)!

In a chemical hood: Needle-shear homogenate with a fresh sterile syringe and a fresh clean 25-gauge needle. Do at least 5 passages of the whole homogenate. Be careful not to puncture the bottom of the tube if using sharp needles.
OPTIONAL: Pellet algae and debris at 5,000 ×g for 5 min at RT. This step if only necessary if using symbiotic anemones and trying to remove most algae before DNA extraction.
Store the samples at −20°C or use them directly.
Add 50 µL 10 mM Tris-Cl, pH 8.5 to the pellet.

Air-dry for 10 min or until no ethanol is visible at the bottom and the walls of the tube. Placing the open tubes in a heat block at 37–42°C accelerates the process but might overdry the DNA making it harder to dissolve again.
Remove the supernatant carefully with a pipet, without touching the area where the pellet should be.
Add 500 µL 70% ethanol, don’t mix, centrifuge at ≥16,000 ×g for 3-5 min at 25°C.

Decant supernatant. A tiny pellet might be visible for big anemones, but probably not if starting with small to medium-sized animals. Just be careful to not scratch off the DNA from the side where the pellet should be.

Centrifuge at ≥16,000 ×g for ≥30 min at 25°C to precipitate the DNA. Be sure to orient all tubes in the same direction to know on which side the pellet will form (bottom side of the tube which faces out- and upwards in the centrifuge rotor).
Incubate for ≥30 min at RT.
Add 10 µL of 3 M NH4Ac (pH 7), mix well.
In a chemical hood: Take 450 µL of the aqueous, upper phase and transfer to a new tube. Do NOT disturb the interphase!
Centrifuge at ≥16,000 ×g for 10 min at 25°C to separate the phases.
In a chemical hood: Add 500 µL of chloroform, mix thoroughly by vortexing several seconds.
In a chemical hood: Take 500 µL of the aqueous, upper phase without disturbing the interphase and transfer to a new tube. Do NOT disturb the interphase!
Centrifuge at ≥16,000 ×g for 10 min at 25°C to separate the phases.
In a chemical hood: Add 500 µL phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1, TE-buffered to pH ≥ 7, very TOXIC!!! ), mix thoroughly by vortexing several seconds.
Add 5 µL RNase A (from a DNase-free 10 mg/mL stock), mix well and incubate 30 min at 37°C.
In a chemical hood: Take 550 µL of the aqueous, upper phase without disturbing the interphase and transfer to a new tube.
Centrifuge at ≥16,000 ×g for 10 min at 25°C to separate the phases.
In a chemical hood: Add 600 µL phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol [25:24:1; saturated with 10 mM Tris-Cl, pH 8; 1 mM EDTA; very TOXIC!!!). Before the next step, make sure the tube is closed tightly.
Let the samples cool down to RT.
Incubate at 65°C for 30 min or longer until most of the solution has cleared. Invert occasionally while incubating.
In a chemical hood: Add 3.6 µL of nuclease-free Proteinase K (20 mg/mL and ≥400 U/mL). Mix by inverting several times.
Vortex at ≥2,000 rpm for 5 min at 4°C. Do not vortex for more than 10 min as that might fragment the DNA too much.
Incubate 10 min at RT to re-dissolve the DNA. Incubation at 37°C and shaking will speed-up the process.
Add 1 mL of 100% ethanol and mix well.
Mix thoroughly by vortexing several seconds. This should result in a milky emulsion with two phases starting to form.
OPTIONAL: For sensitive applications, the chloroform wash step can be repeated to remove any trace amounts of phenol from the sample.
creator: Christian CR Renicke
